basics.name: Shiping Gao
basics.label: PhD Student in Computer Science and Engineering
basics.summary: PhD student in Computer Science and Engineering at the University of Michigan. Research interests include NLP, LLMs, RLHF, reward hacking, fine-grained reward modeling, and LLM reasoning.

work.summary: Unleashing Implicit Rewards: Prefix-Value Learning for Distribution-Level Optimization. Supervised by Prof. Lifu Huang.
highlights: Developed IPVRM with value-function-style supervision on every prefix.
Introduced distribution-level temporal-difference advantages for dense credit assignment.

work.summary: Discriminative Policy Optimization for Token-Level Reward Models. Supervised by Prof. Xiaojun Quan.
highlights: Designed Q-RM, a token-level reward model without fine-grained annotations.
Integrated Q-RM into PPO and REINFORCE for reasoning tasks.

work.summary: Advantage-Guided Distillation for Preference Alignment in Small Language Models. Supervised by Prof. Xiaojun Quan.
highlights: Designed DCKD, ADPA, and ADPA+ for small-model preference alignment.
Improved alignment performance while reducing sample complexity.

work.summary: Edit-Wise Preference Optimization for Grammatical Error Correction. Supervised by Prof. Xiaojun Quan.
highlights: Proposed EPO with edit-aware token weighting.
Built annotation-free preference data construction for GEC.

work.summary: Research on Chinese Text Correction System Based on Deep Learning. Supervised by Prof. Binbin Yong.
highlights: Designed a Chinese text correction system for spelling, grammar, and semantic errors.
Proposed AutoCscError for realistic pseudo-data generation.

work.summary: A Novel Dynamic Interpolation Method Based on Temporal and Spatial Correlations. Supervised by Prof. Zhili Zhao.
highlights: Proposed dynamic spatiotemporal interpolation for environmental and meteorological monitoring.

education.area: PhD in Computer Science and Engineering
